title: The Nitrogen Cycle
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Step-through p5.js animation tracing nitrogen through fixation, nitrification, assimilation, decomposition, and denitrification with labeled organisms

learningObjectives: Trace nitrogen through fixation, nitrification, assimilation, and denitrification stages
Identify the organisms involved at each step of the nitrogen cycle
Predict the environmental consequences of adding excess nitrogen fertilizer
prerequisites: Understanding of producers, consumers, and decomposers

keyQuestions: Why can't most organisms use atmospheric nitrogen (N2) directly?
What role do bacteria play in making nitrogen available to plants?
How does adding fertilizer disrupt the natural nitrogen cycle?
Why is denitrification important for completing the cycle?
commonMisconceptions: Plants absorb nitrogen directly from the air -- in reality, atmospheric N2 must be fixed by bacteria before plants can use it
Nitrogen fixation is the only way nitrogen enters the soil -- decomposition of organic matter also releases usable nitrogen
Fertilizer is the same as what bacteria produce -- synthetic fertilizers bypass the biological cycle and can overwhelm natural processing capacity
assessmentOpportunities: Have students draw the nitrogen cycle from memory after the step-through and compare to the simulation
Ask students to trace a single nitrogen atom through the complete cycle naming each process
Connect the fertilizer experiment to real-world eutrophication case studies
